name: Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - Security
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - Security
SIGMA Description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts

False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4697 AND (LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%mshta%' AND LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%vbscript:createobject%' AND LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%.run%' AND LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%window.close%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - Security on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4697 AND (LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%mshta%' AND LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%vbscript:createobject%' AND LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%.run%' AND LOWER("Service Filename") LIKE '%window.close%') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - Security

Event description: Converted from SIGMA Rule. Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Event Name
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
